clusters.label: A
clusters.summary: Two high-risk hexagons are identified in the Southern Central Valley, with an average probability of 10.35% and a maximum probability of 10.94%. The area is experiencing high temperatures, with a 7-day average daytime surface temperature of 43.5°C and a drought index of 7.35. CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO protocols are in effect, with a recommendation to be prepared to leave immediately.
actions: Position Type 1 engines within 15-min response time of high-risk areas.
Activate aerial resources on standby at nearest air base.
Deploy water tenders at key highway corridors.

overall_assessment: Seven active clusters are identified across California, with a total of 43 high-risk hexagons. The areas are experiencing high temperatures, drought conditions, and increased fire risk. CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO protocols are in effect for all high-risk areas, with a recommendation to be prepared to leave immediately.
